item.title: Games Industry Faces More Painful Months Ahead
item.description: The world’s biggest games publisher is discussing axing some investments

item.title: British Retailers Brace for Fallout From EU’s New E-commerce Customs Fees
item.description: Big economies like the US and EU are ending tariff-free exemptions for e-commerce

item.title: Greenspan Reflections, From ‘Alan Greenstud’ to a ‘Flaw’ in His Model
item.description: As Kevin Warsh says AI will allow faster growth, the late Fed chair’s legacy is worth revisiting.

item.title: Abu Dhabi’s MGX Raises About $50 Billion to Accelerate AI Deals
item.description: Abu Dhabi’s MGX has raised close to $50 billion from regional and global investors to accelerate spending on artificial intelligence infrastructure and technology, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Getty Just Became an AI Company. It Now Has to Perform Like One
item.description: The stock-image giant tried home-grown AI before its tie-up with OpenAI.

item.title: Digital Euro Clears Key Hurdle to Rollout in EU Parliament
item.description: European Union lawmakers cleared the way for talks on the legal framework that will underpin the digital euro, a project deemed vital to the bloc’s monetary independence.

item.title: NYC Votes in Lively Primaries, With Suburban Race Key to House
item.description: New York City is living through the noisiest Congressional primary season in a generation.

item.title: Tech Losses Pile Up as Investors Focus on AI Risks
item.description: Investors are focusing on AI risks and potential interest rate hikes.

item.title: BBVA Offloads AI Risk With $2 Billion Infrastructure-Linked SRT
item.description: BBVA SA is close to completing a significant risk transfer to hedge some of its rapidly-expanding portfolio of artificial intelligence-related lending.

item.title: Google Taps Employee Alumni Network With AI Startup Incubator
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google is backing a new incubator for former employees building artificial intelligence startups, deepening its ties with one of Silicon Valley’s largest and most influential alumni groups.

item.title: US Workers Lose Buying Power After War Drives Up Prices
item.description: Whether they work in restaurants, offices or warehouses, Americans have seen their paycheck eaten up by a surge in everyday costs recently.

item.title: The Growing Crisis for America's Child Abuse Investigators
item.description: With the proliferation of AI-generated abuse material, police and investigators aren't getting the robust mental health support they need.

item.title: Bristow Is Said to Near Berry Aviation Deal in Government Push
item.description: Bristow Group Inc. is nearing an acquisition of privately held Berry Aviation Inc. as it seeks to expand in the government services sector, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: High Frequency Traders Expand Push Into Currency Options Market
item.description: High-speed trading firms are making fresh inroads into the currency options market, with IMC Trading and Susquehanna International Group expanding globally and Optiver Holding pursuing more complex offerings.

item.title: EU to Escalate Meta Probe Into Addictive Design That Hooks Kids
item.description: The European Union is set to ramp up an investigation into Meta Platforms Inc. that alleges its products are addictive to children, as the bloc increases regulatory pressure on the US social media company.

item.title: Euro Hits August Low as Lagarde’s Rates Talk Diverges From Fed
item.description: The euro fell to its lowest level since August after weak economic data and dovish comments from European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde prompted traders to pare back their bets on interest-rate hikes.

item.title: Sturgeon’s Estranged Husband Gets 5 Years for Embezzlement
item.description: The Scottish National Party’s former chief executive — who’s also the estranged husband of ex-leader Nicola Sturgeon — was sentenced to five years and three months in prison after admitting to embezzling more than £400,000 ($529,000).

item.title: AllianzGI Gets $744 Million for Latest APAC Private Credit Fund
item.description: Allianz Global Investors secured $744 Million in commitments for the first close of its latest private credit fund targeting investments in the Asia Pacific, underscoring sustained investor interest for the asset class in the region.

item.title: SoftBank-Backed Car Marketplace Carro Is Said to Explore US IPO
item.description: Used-car marketplace Carro is considering filing confidentially for an initial public offering in the US as soon as this month, people familiar with the matter said, testing investor appetite for Southeast Asian startups.

item.title: SpaceX Rout Set to Push Market Value Below $2 Trillion
item.description: SpaceX shares extended declines in premarket trading, a day after the company saw the second-largest single-session drop in market value on record.

item.title: ECB’s Escrivá Says Oil Starting to Ripple Through Other Sectors
item.description: European Central Bank Governing Council member Jose Luis Escrivá said high oil prices are feeding through to other parts of the economy.

item.title: ECB’s Lane Sees Danger Inflation Will Top 2% for Some Time
item.description: European Central Bank officials are facing the risk that inflation will hover above their goal “for quite some time,” according to Chief Economist Philip Lane.

item.title: UK Economy Shrinks for Second Straight Month, Flash PMI Shows
item.description: The UK economy shrank for a second consecutive month in June, according to a prominent private sector survey that showed firms cutting more jobs.

item.title: South African Consumer Mood Darkens as Iran War Hits Fuel Prices
item.description: South African consumer confidence fell sharply in the second quarter as households confronted a sharp rise in fuel prices caused by fallout from the Iran war.

item.title: Indonesia Orders Miners to Provide More Coal Amid Blackouts
item.description: Indonesia’s energy ministry directed miners to urgently boost domestic coal supplies just before the country was hit by rolling blackouts, the latest strain on President Prabowo Subianto’s administration following a week of protests.

item.title: Euro-Zone Business Activity Shows Resilience to Iran War Fallout
item.description: Business activity in the euro area shrank less than anticipated in June, feeding optimism that the region’s economy can weather faster inflation and the jolt to confidence dealt by the Iran war.

item.title: IG4 Says It Can Buy All Raizen Debt in Cash to Gain Control
item.description: Private equity firm IG4 Capital said it has enough capital to buy Raizen SA debt entirely in cash if needed as it seeks to acquire enough of the troubled sugar and ethanol producer’s obligations to end up with a 50.1% equity stake.

item.title: Hormuz Traffic Picks Up as More Tankers Broadcast Crossings
item.description: More ships are openly signaling their intention to traverse the Strait of Hormuz, pointing to growing confidence among shipowners and traders about sending vessels through the chokepoint as tensions ease.

item.title: German Business Activity Unexpectedly Worsens on Services Slump
item.description: Germany’s private sector shrank for a third month as sagging confidence over the Iran war sent services activity sinking at its fastest pace since 2022.

item.title: Prosus Develops OpenClaw Rival to Address Privacy Concerns
item.description: Prosus NV has developed its own version of the artificial intelligence agent OpenClaw to get around European concerns over data privacy, and will start rolling it out this week.
